content:
Build an invoice form with dynamic line items using Nuxt UI's nested form support.

Requirements:

1. Create a parent `UForm` with:
   - **Customer** field (`UInput`, name: `customer`)
   - **Date** field (`UInput` with type="date", name: `date`)
2. Below the header fields, render a dynamic list of line items where users can add/remove rows
3. Each line item must be a **nested `UForm`** with:
   - `nested` prop to link validation with the parent form
   - `name` prop using the pattern `items.${index}` to inherit state from parent
   - **Description** field (`UInput`, name: `description`)
   - **Quantity** field (`UInputNumber`, name: `quantity`)
   - **Price** field (`UInputNumber`, name: `price`)
4. Add an "Add Item" button to dynamically push new items
5. Add a "Remove" button per row
6. Use Zod schemas for both the parent form and nested item validation
7. Submit should log the complete form state including all items

The key requirement is using Nuxt UI v4's nested form pattern with `nested` prop and `name` inheritance — nested forms must NOT have their own `:state` prop.

input.prompt: Extract all information about nested forms, the nested prop, name prop inheritance, how to use UForm with nested child UForm components, and the pattern for dynamic list items. Include code examples.

content.content:
# Nested Forms in UForm

## Core Concept

The `nested` prop enables multiple Form components to work together hierarchically. According to the documentation, "validating the parent form will automatically validate all the other forms inside it."

Child forms directly inherit their parent's state, eliminating the need for separate state objects. The `name` prop targets nested attributes within the parent's state.

## Key Props

**`nested`**: Boolean flag that "attach[es] to its parent Form and validate[s] at the same time."

**`name`**: Available only when `nested` is true, this prop specifies "the path of the form's state within its parent form."

## Use Case: Conditional Fields

When a user's selection determines field visibility, nested forms handle dynamic validation:

```vue
<UForm :state="state" :schema="schema" @submit="onSubmit">
  <UFormField label="Name" name="name">
    <UInput v-model="state.name" />
  </UFormField>

  <UCheckbox v-model="state.news" label="Newsletter signup" />

  <!-- Nested form appears conditionally -->
  <UForm v-if="state.news" :schema="nestedSchema" nested>
    <UFormField label="Email" name="email">
      <UInput v-model="state.email" />
    </UFormField>
  </UForm>

  <UButton type="submit">Submit</UButton>
</UForm>
```

## Use Case: Dynamic List Items

For repeating field groups, iterate with indexed `name` props:

```vue
<UForm :state="state" :schema="schema" @submit="onSubmit">
  <UFormField label="Customer" name="customer">
    <UInput v-model="state.customer" />
  </UFormField>

  <UForm
    v-for="(item, count) in state.items"
    :key="count"
    :name="`items.${count}`"
    :schema="itemSchema"
    nested
  >
    <UFormField label="Description" name="description">
      <UInput v-model="item.description" />
    </UFormField>
    <UFormField label="Price" name="price">
      <UInput v-model="item.price" type="number" />
    </UFormField>
  </UForm>

  <UButton @click="addItem">Add Item</UButton>
  <UButton type="submit">Submit</UButton>
</UForm>
```

The indexed path syntax (`items.0`, `items.1`) enables validation of array elements using dot notation in your schema.

# Nested Forms in UForm

## Core Concept

The `nested` prop enables multiple Form components to work together hierarchically. According to the documentation, "validating the parent form will automatically validate all the other forms inside it."

Child forms directly inherit their parent's state, eliminating the need for separate state objects. The `name` prop targets nested attributes within the parent's state.

## Key Props

**`nested`**: Boolean flag that "attach[es] to its parent Form and validate[s] at the same time."

**`name`**: Available only when `nested` is true, this prop specifies "the path of the form's state within its parent form."

## Use Case: Conditional Fields

When a user's selection determines field visibility, nested forms handle dynamic validation:

```vue
<UForm :state="state" :schema="schema" @submit="onSubmit">
  <UFormField label="Name" name="name">
    <UInput v-model="state.name" />
  </UFormField>

  <UCheckbox v-model="state.news" label="Newsletter signup" />

  <!-- Nested form appears conditionally -->
  <UForm v-if="state.news" :schema="nestedSchema" nested>
    <UFormField label="Email" name="email">
      <UInput v-model="state.email" />
    </UFormField>
  </UForm>

</UForm>
```

## Use Case: Dynamic List Items

For repeating field groups, iterate with indexed `name` props:

```vue
<UForm :state="state" :schema="schema" @submit="onSubmit">
  <UFormField label="Customer" name="customer">
    <UInput v-model="state.customer" />
  </UFormField>

  <UForm
    v-for="(item, count) in state.items"
    :key="count"
    :name="`items.${count}`"
    :schema="itemSchema"
    nested
  >
    <UFormField label="Description" name="description">
      <UInput v-model="item.description" />
    </UFormField>
    <UFormField label="Price" name="price">
      <UInput v-model="item.price" type="number" />
    </UFormField>
  </UForm>

  <UButton @click="addItem">Add Item</UButton>
  <UButton type="submit">Submit</UButton>
</UForm>
```

The indexed path syntax (`items.0`, `items.1`) enables validation of array elements using dot notation in your schema.

content.text:
Done. Here's what was built:

**`app/pages/index.vue`** — the complete invoice form.

### Key patterns used

| Pattern | Detail |
|---|---|
| Parent `UForm` | Has `:state` and `:schema` — owns the reactive state |
| Nested `UForm` | Has `nested` prop + `:name="\`items.${index}\`"` — **no `:state`** |
| Zod schemas | `itemSchema` for each row, `schema` for the full invoice |
| Dynamic rows | `state.items` array driven with `push` / `splice` |

### Structure
- **Header**: `customer` (`UInput`) + `date` (`UInput type="date"`)
- **Line items**: each row is a nested `UForm` with `description` (`UInput`), `quantity` (`UInputNumber`), `price` (`UInputNumber`), and a trash icon remove button (disabled when only 1 row remains)
- **Footer**: live total computed from `quantity × price`, plus the submit button
- Submitting logs the full state via `console.log`
